name: Zaporizhian Sich (c. 1556–1775)

colors.symbolism: the crimson silk of the historic Cossack khoruhva banner
the gold figure of the Archangel Michael, patron of the Sich

description: A crimson field charged with the gold figure of the Archangel Michael — a reconstructed depiction of the khoruhva (regimental banner) of the Zaporizhian Sich.
history: The Zaporizhian Sich was the self-governing Cossack republic of the lower Dnipro, the military and political heart of the Ukrainian Cossack movement from the mid-16th century until its abolition by Catherine II in 1775. Its banners (khoruhvy) were crimson silk bearing sacred imagery, most commonly the Archangel Michael (the patron of Kyiv and of the Sich) flanked by the Saviour. The depiction here is a modern heraldic reconstruction; surviving Sich banners are diverse and personal to particular hetmans or kurins.

features.symbolism: the patron saint of Kyiv and of the Zaporizhian Sich
